Holiday and Festival Working Engineering and IT personnel rostered for production support or on-call duty during a gazetted holiday will be granted compensatory off, to be availed within 30 days, subject to manager approval and recorded through the self-service portal. 9. Advance Leave In exceptional circumstances, an employee who has been confirmed in service may be granted Earned Leave in advance of accrual, up to a maximum of five days, against future accrual. Advance Leave requires Department Head approval and will be adjusted automatically against subsequent monthly accruals. 10. Queries and Contact For clarifications regarding leave balances, encashment calculations, statutory entitlements or portal issues, employees may contact the Human Resources team at hr@infovance.com or visit the HR Operations desk on the 4th floor during working hours. This policy supersedes all previous leave policies and is subject to annual review.

question: How long is the probation period for a new employee, and under the security policy how often must they rotate their password?
ground_truth: A new employee serves a probation period of six months from the date of joining (per the Employee Onboarding Guide). Under the Information Security Policy, passwords must be rotated every 90 days.
answer: The probation period for a new employee is six months from the date of joining [Source: 1]. Under the Information Security Policy, employees must rotate their passwords every 90 days [Source: 2].
contexts: New Employee Onboarding Guide Human Resources Department - Infovance Technologies Pvt. Ltd. Document ID: HR-ONB-GD-2025-02 Version: 2.5 Effective: 01 April 2025 Owner: Human Resources Department Next review: 31 March 2026 1. Welcome to Infovance Technologies Welcome aboard! This guide is designed to help you settle in quickly and become a productive, confident member of the Infovance family. Founded in 2015, Infovance Technologies is a mid-sized IT services and consulting company headquartered in Noida, Uttar Pradesh, serving enterprise clients across banking, manufacturing, retail and healthcare. With around 800 colleagues across our Human Resources, Information Technology, Finance and Engineering departments, we pride ourselves on engineering excellence, integrity and a culture of continuous learning. Over the coming days, your manager, your buddy and the HR team will guide you through everything you need to start strong. Our way of working is grounded in a small set of values. Integrity means we are honest with clients and colleagues and do the right thing even when no one is watching. Craftsmanship means we care about the quality of what we build and favour deep understanding over shortcuts. Ownership means we follow problems through to resolution rather than passing them along. Respect means we listen, assume good intent, and value the diverse backgrounds of our colleagues. Learning means we stay curious and help one another grow. You will see these values reflected in how we run meetings, give feedback and make decisions, and you are encouraged to hold us to them from your very first week. Your onboarding buddy is your informal go-to for the small questions that do not warrant a formal ticket — from where to find the best coffee to how a particular tool is used day to day. 2. Day 1 Checklist Your first day is about getting set up and getting to know the people around you. Please ensure the following are completed before the end of Day 1: Report to the HR Operations desk on the 4th floor by 9:30 AM and complete joining formalities and document verification. Collect your photo ID access card and have your biometric attendance enrolled. Receive your company laptop from the IT Helpdesk and verify it powers on and is encrypted. Activate your corporate accounts: email, single sign-on, and the InfoPeople self-service portal. Set up multi-factor authentication using the Microsoft Authenticator app. Sign and submit the Code of Conduct, the Information Security Policy acknowledgement and the confidentiality undertaking. Meet your reporting manager and assigned onboarding buddy. Submit bank-account and PAN details for payroll, and nominate beneficiaries for Provident Fund and gratuity. 3. Week 1 Schedule Your first week blends orientation with early integration into your team. Indicative schedule (your manager may adjust Monday Joining formalities, IT setup, welcome lunch HR / IT Helpdesk Tuesday Company overview, values, org structure, HR policies HR Business Partner Wednesday Information security & compliance training InfoSec team Thursday Department deep-dive and tools walkthrough Reporting Manager Friday Team introductions, first task assignment, buddy check-in Team / Buddy 4. IT Setup Instructions The IT Helpdesk will help you configure your workstation. Please complete the following in your first two days: Email: Sign in to Outlook with your firstname.lastname@infovance.com address and complete MFA enrolment. Slack: Join the workspace at infovance.slack.com and add yourself to #announcements, #helpdesk and your team channel. Jira: Log in to jira.infovance.com to access project boards and the Service Desk portal. VPN: Install Cisco AnyConnect and connect to vpn.infovance.com to verify remote access works before any work-from-home day. Confluence: Bookmark the engineering and HR knowledge bases. Raise any setup issues at helpdesk@infovance.com or through the Jira Service Desk. 5. HR Processes You Will Use 5.1 Timesheet Submission Timesheets are submitted weekly through the InfoPeople portal and must be approved by your manager by 12:00 noon every Monday for the previous week. Accurate timesheets are essential for client billing and project reporting. 5.2 Expense Claims Business expenses are claimed through the portal under Finance → Reimbursements, with bills attached, within 30 days of the expense. Refer to the Expense Reimbursement Policy for limits and eligibility. 5.3 Leave Portal Apply for leave through the portal under Leave & Attendance. Your leave balances accrue monthly; please plan planned leave at least seven working days in advance. 6. Benefits Overview Group Health Insurance: Floater cover of INR 5,00,000 covering you, your spouse and up to two children, with cashless treatment at network hospitals. Parents may be added on a voluntary, employee-funded basis. Provident Fund (PF): Statutory contribution of 12% of basic salary by both employee and employer, managed through the EPFO, with an associated Universal Account Number (UAN). Gratuity: Payable as per the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972, on completion of five years of continuous service. Group Term Life and Personal Accident cover for added financial protection. Annual health check-up, employee assistance programme and learning stipend. 7. Key Contacts Function Contact Channel Human Resources hr@infovance.com 4th floor / portal IT Helpdesk helpdesk@infovance.com Jira Service Desk Information Security security@infovance.com Hotline ext. 4000 Finance / Payroll finance@infovance.com 3rd floor Facilities & Admin admin@infovance.com Ground floor 8. Office Locations and Timings The corporate headquarters is located in Sector 62, Noida, Uttar Pradesh. Standard office hours are 9:30 AM to 6:30 PM, Monday to Friday, with a one-hour lunch break and flexible start windows for eligible roles. The office operates a hybrid model; the number of in-office days is determined at the team level. Access is via your photo ID card; visitors must be registered with reception in advance. 9. Important Policies to Read in Your First Week Code of Conduct and Anti-Harassment (POSH) Policy. Information Security Policy (mandatory acknowledgement). Employee Leave Policy — FY 2025-26. Expense Reimbursement Policy — FY 2025-26. Acceptable Use and Remote Work guidelines. 10. Probation Period New employees serve a probation period of six months from the date of joining. During probation, your manager will set clear objectives and provide regular feedback. A mid-probation check-in is held at the three-month mark. On successful completion, you will receive a written confirmation of employment. The probation period may be extended once, by up to three months, where additional development time is warranted, with documented feedback. 11. Performance Review Cycle and an annual appraisal in March–April. Reviews are based on goals agreed at the start of the cycle, demonstrated competencies and our core values. Continuous feedback is encouraged throughout the year; you do not need to wait for a formal cycle to seek or give feedback. Increments and promotions, where applicable, take effect from the start of the new financial year. For any onboarding questions, reach out to hr@infovance.com — we are glad to have you with us.

Information Security Policy — 2025 Information Security & Risk - Infovance Technologies Pvt. Ltd. Document ID: IT-SEC-POL-2025-01 Version: 4.0 Effective: 01 January 2025 Owner: Information Security & Risk Next review: 31 December 2025 1. Introduction and Objective Information is among the most valuable assets of Infovance Technologies Pvt. Ltd. This Information Security Policy establishes the minimum mandatory controls that protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of Company and client information across all systems, networks, devices and physical locations. It applies to every employee, contractor, intern and third party who accesses Company information assets, regardless of location or device. Adherence is a condition of continued access to Company systems. This policy is aligned with the principles of ISO/IEC 27001 and references the obligations arising under the Information Technology Act, 2000 (including the Reasonable Security Practices under Section 43A) and the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, as applicable to the personal data the Company processes on behalf of its clients. 2. Password and Authentication Policy Strong authentication is the first line of defence against unauthorised access. All users must comply with the following password standards for Company accounts, including email, VPN, the InfoPeople portal and engineering systems: Minimum length of 12 characters. Must include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeral and one special character (for example, ! @ # $ % & *). Must not contain the user’s name, employee ID, date of birth, or the word ‘infovance’ in any case variation. Mandatory rotation every 90 days; the system will prompt at day 83 and enforce at day 90. Re-use of any of the last 8 passwords is prohibited. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) via the Microsoft Authenticator app is mandatory for email, VPN and all administrative consoles. Passwords must never be shared, written down in plain text, or stored in browsers on shared machines. Use the Company-approved password manager (Keeper) instead. Accounts are locked after five consecutive failed login attempts and can be unlocked via self-service or by raising a ticket with the IT Helpdesk. 3. Device Usage Policy 3.1 Company-Issued Devices Company laptops are issued for business use and remain Company property. They are centrally managed through Microsoft Intune, with disk encryption (BitLocker), endpoint protection (CrowdStrike Falcon) and automatic patching enabled. Users must not disable security agents, install unlicensed software, or alter system configurations. A screen lock with a timeout of 5 minutes of inactivity is enforced on all devices and may not be extended. 3.2 Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Personal devices may access only Company email and approved collaboration apps, and only after enrolment in mobile device management. BYOD devices must run a supported operating system, have device encryption and a screen passcode enabled, and accept the Company’s remote-wipe capability for corporate data. Personal devices must not be used to store Confidential or Restricted data locally. 4. Remote Access and VPN Usage Connection to the Company network from any location outside the office must be made through the corporate VPN. The use of the VPN is mandatory for all remote work. Key requirements are: VPN client: Cisco AnyConnect Secure Mobility Client (latest approved version). VPN gateway: vpn.infovance.com. Authentication uses corporate credentials plus MFA. Split-tunnelling is disabled; all traffic is routed through the Company gateway while connected. Idle VPN sessions are disconnected automatically after 30 minutes of inactivity. 5. Data Classification All information must be classified so that the appropriate level of protection is applied. Owners are responsible for classifying the data they create or handle. Classification Description Examples Handling Requirement Approved for public release Marketing brochures, press releases No restriction; verify approval before release For internal business use Org charts, internal memos, project plans Share only within the Company Sensitive business or client data Client deliverables, contracts, source code Need-to-know; encrypt in transit and at rest Highest sensitivity Personal data, credentials, financial records, M&A data Strict need-to-know; encryption + access logging 6. Email and Messaging Security Corporate email is provided for business purposes and is monitored for security and compliance. The following controls Company email must not be auto-forwarded to personal email accounts (for example, Gmail, Yahoo, Outlook.com). Forwarding rules to external addresses are blocked. with a separately communicated password. Be alert to phishing: verify unexpected requests for credentials, payments or data through a second channel before acting. Do not click links or open attachments from unknown or unverified senders; report them using the ‘Report Phish’ button in Outlook. Mass external mailings must be routed through the approved marketing platform, never the personal mailbox. 7. Security Incident Reporting Prompt reporting limits the impact of a security incident. A security incident includes (but is not limited to) lost or stolen devices, suspected malware, phishing compromise, unauthorised access, accidental data disclosure, or any event that may affect the confidentiality, integrity or availability of Company or client data. Report all suspected or confirmed incidents to security@infovance.com within 2 hours of discovery. For critical incidents (active breach, ransomware, large-scale data exposure), also call the Security Operations hotline at extension 4000. Do not attempt to investigate or remediate on your own; preserve evidence and await instructions from the Information Security team. Do not discuss the incident on public channels or with external parties. 8. Prohibited Activities The following activities are strictly prohibited on Company systems and networks: Sharing credentials or allowing another person to use your account. Installing pirated, unlicensed or unauthorised software. Disabling, bypassing or tampering with security controls (antivirus, encryption, firewall, VPN, logging). Copying Confidential or Restricted data to personal cloud storage or removable media. Accessing data or systems for which you have no business need. Connecting unauthorised devices (rogue access points, personal servers) to the corporate network. Using Company assets for illegal activity, harassment, or to access prohibited content. Mining cryptocurrency or running unauthorised commercial workloads on Company infrastructure. 9. Consequences of Policy Violation Violations of this policy are taken seriously and may result in disciplinary action proportionate to the severity and intent of the breach. Depending on the circumstances, consequences may include a formal written warning, suspension of system access, withholding of increment or promotion, termination of employment, and, where laws have been broken, civil or criminal proceedings. Where a violation also breaches a client contract, the Company may be obliged to notify the affected client. All disciplinary actions follow the principles of natural justice and the Company’s disciplinary procedure. 10. Acknowledgement and Review Every employee must read and acknowledge this policy during onboarding and at each annual refresh through the InfoPeople portal. The Information Security & Risk function reviews this policy at least annually, or sooner in response to material changes in the threat landscape, technology or regulation. Questions may be directed to security@infovance.com.

IT Helpdesk Standard Operating Procedure IT Helpdesk & Support - Infovance Technologies Pvt. Ltd. Document ID: IT-SOP-HD-2025-05 Version: 3.4 Effective: 01 April 2025 Owner: IT Helpdesk & Support Next review: 31 March 2026 1. Purpose and Scope This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) defines how the IT Helpdesk at Infovance Technologies receives, prioritises, resolves and escalates technology support requests. It covers all employees across the Human Resources, Information Technology, Finance and Engineering departments at the Noida headquarters and remote workers. The goal is consistent, timely and courteous support that keeps colleagues productive while maintaining security and asset control. 2. How to Raise a Ticket There are two approved channels for support requests. Using these channels ensures your request is tracked, prioritised and resolved within the defined service levels: Email: Send a description of the issue to helpdesk@infovance.com. A ticket is created automatically and a reference number is returned. Self-Service Portal: Raise a request through the Jira Service Desk portal at servicedesk.infovance.com, choosing the relevant request type and priority. Please do not request support through direct personal messages to individual IT staff, as these requests are not tracked and cannot be guaranteed a response time. 3. Priority Levels and Response Targets Each ticket is assigned a priority based on business impact and urgency. The Helpdesk uses the following matrix to set expectations for the first response: Priority Definition Examples Response Target P1 - Critical Major outage or security event affecting many users or a whole service Server down, data breach, core system unavailable 1 hour P2 - High Significant impact to an individual or team; no workaround Email down, VPN not working, cannot log in 4 hours P3 - Medium Moderate impact with a workaround available Software installation, printer issue, access request 1 business day P4 - Low Minor impact or routine request Hardware upgrade request, general query 3 business days Response target is the time to first meaningful response, not necessarily full resolution. P1 incidents trigger immediate notification to the IT Manager and, where security is involved, the Information Security team. 4. Common Issues and Self-Service Solutions Many issues can be resolved quickly by following the steps below before raising a ticket. If these do not resolve the problem, raise a ticket and include what you have already tried. 4.1 VPN Not Connecting Confirm you have a stable internet connection by opening any website. Open Cisco AnyConnect and ensure the gateway is set to vpn.infovance.com. Enter your corporate credentials and approve the multi-factor authentication prompt. If it fails, fully quit and reopen the client, then retry; restart the laptop if needed. Check that your password has not expired (see 4.2). If the issue persists, raise a P2 ticket with a screenshot of the error. 4.2 Password Reset Go to the self-service password portal at reset.infovance.com. Verify your identity using the registered authenticator or recovery method. Choose a new password that meets policy (minimum 12 characters, complexity, not one of the last 8 used). Update the saved password on your devices and VPN client. If you are locked out and cannot self-serve, raise a P2 ticket or call the Helpdesk line. 4.3 New Software Request Raise a P3 request in the Jira Service Desk under ‘Software Request’. Specify the software name, version, business justification and your manager. Manager approval is captured in the workflow; licensed or paid software also needs Finance sign-off. Once approved, IT installs the software remotely or schedules a session; unauthorised self-installation is not permitted. 4.4 Hardware Issue Reporting Note the asset tag on your device and a clear description of the fault. For total failure that stops work, raise a P2 ticket; for non-blocking issues, raise a P3/P4. Attach photos of any physical damage or error messages. IT will triage for repair, replacement or a temporary loaner device. 5. Escalation Matrix If a ticket is not progressing within its service level, or its impact increases, it is escalated as follows: Level Owner When Helpdesk Support Engineer First response, triage and common fixes Senior Support / Systems Engineer Unresolved at L1 or specialised issue Infrastructure / Application Specialist Complex, root-cause or server-side issues Management IT Manager P1 incidents, SLA breaches, or user escalation 6. Asset Management IT maintains a central asset register for all laptops, peripherals and licensed equipment. Key processes are: Assignment: Each device is tagged and recorded against the employee at issue; the employee acknowledges receipt in the asset system. In-service: Employees are responsible for the safe custody of assigned assets; loss or damage must be reported immediately. Return on exit: All assets must be returned to IT during the exit/clearance process. The IT clearance sign-off is mandatory for final settlement. Periodic asset audits are conducted to reconcile the register with devices in use. 7. Software License Management IT centrally tracks software licences to ensure compliance and cost control. Licences are allocated per user or per device and reclaimed when no longer needed (for example, on role change or exit). Only software procured and approved through IT may be installed on Company devices; use of unlicensed software exposes the Company to legal and security risk and is prohibited under the Information Security Policy. Renewal of critical licences is tracked in advance to avoid service interruption. 8. Remote Support Procedure For issues that require IT to view or control your screen, remote support is provided using the approved tool, TeamViewer. The procedure is: The support engineer asks for your TeamViewer ID and the session passcode displayed on your screen. Share these only with a verified Helpdesk engineer working on your active ticket. You retain control and can observe all actions; the session ends when you close TeamViewer. Never share remote-access details with anyone claiming to be ‘IT’ outside of an open ticket — this is a common social-engineering tactic; report such attempts to security@infovance.com. 9. After-Hours Emergency Support The Helpdesk operates during standard business hours (9:00 AM to 7:00 PM, Monday to Friday). For P1 critical incidents outside these hours — such as a major outage or a suspected security breach — contact the on-call IT emergency line at +91-120-4XXXXXX (ext. 4111), monitored 24x7. Security incidents must also be reported to security@infovance.com within two hours of discovery, per the Information Security Policy. 10. Continuous Improvement Ticket trends, recurring issues and satisfaction ratings are reviewed monthly to identify automation opportunities, knowledge-base additions and training needs. Employees are encouraged to rate resolved tickets and suggest improvements. For feedback on IT support, write to helpdesk@infovance.com. This SOP is reviewed annually and updated as tools and processes evolve.

Employee Leave Policy — FY 2025-26 Human Resources Department - Infovance Technologies Pvt. Ltd. Document ID: HR-POL-LV-2025-06 Version: 3.2 Effective: 01 April 2025 Owner: Human Resources Department Next review: 31 March 2026 1. Purpose and Scope This Employee Leave Policy governs all categories of paid and unpaid leave available to employees of Infovance Technologies Pvt. Ltd. (“the Company”) for the financial year 1 April 2025 to 31 March 2026. It applies to all full-time employees on the Company rolls across the Human Resources, Information Technology, Finance and Engineering departments at the Noida headquarters and any associated delivery centres. The policy is framed in alignment with the applicable provisions of the Uttar Pradesh Shops and Commercial Establishments Act, the Maternity Benefit Act, 1961 (as amended by the Maternity Benefit (Amendment) Act, 2017), and the Company’s internal service rules. Interns, contractors, retainers and third-party vendor personnel are not covered by this policy and are governed by the terms of their respective engagement agreements. In the event of any conflict between this policy and a specific clause of an individual employment contract, the more favourable interpretation to the employee shall ordinarily apply, subject to review by the Head of Human Resources. 2. Leave Year and Accrual The leave year corresponds to the financial year (April to March). Earned Leave and Casual Leave accrue on a pro-rata monthly basis from the date of joining. Employees who join mid-year will have their entitlement calculated proportionately to the number of completed months of service in the leave year. Leave cannot be availed before it is accrued, except where the Advance Leave provisions in Section 9 apply. 3. Categories of Leave and Annual Entitlement Leave Type Annual Entitlement Carry Forward Encashable Notice Required Casual Leave (CL) 12 days Not allowed (lapses) No 1 working day Earned Leave (EL) 15 days Up to 45 days Yes (on EL above 15) 7 working days Sick Leave (SL) 10 days Up to 15 days No Same day / on return Maternity Leave 26 weeks Not applicable No 8 weeks prior (where possible) Paternity Leave 5 days Not applicable No 2 weeks prior (where possible) Bereavement Leave 3 days per event Not applicable No On occurrence Optional Holidays 2 days Not allowed (lapses) No 3 working days 3.1 Casual Leave (12 days) Casual Leave is intended for short, unforeseen personal requirements such as attending to urgent personal work, minor illness not requiring medical certification, or family commitments. CL may be availed for a maximum of three consecutive days at a time. Casual Leave cannot be clubbed with Earned Leave or Sick Leave. Unused Casual Leave lapses at the end of the leave year and is neither carried forward nor encashed. Applications should reach the reporting manager at least one working day in advance, except in genuine emergencies. 3.2 Earned Leave (15 days) Earned Leave (also referred to as Privilege Leave) is intended for planned absences such as vacations or extended personal travel. EL must be applied for at least seven working days in advance to allow for resourcing adjustments. A maximum of 45 days of Earned Leave may be carried forward into the next leave year; any balance above 45 days will lapse unless it has been encashed. Earned Leave above the 15-day annual entitlement may be encashed once per year during the March payroll cycle at the rate of (Basic + Dearness Allowance) / 30 per day. 3.3 Sick Leave (10 days) Sick Leave is granted for absence on medical grounds. For any sickness absence of three or more continuous days, a medical certificate from a registered medical practitioner must be submitted to the HR team within three working days of returning to work. Up to 15 days of unused Sick Leave may be carried forward; Sick Leave is not encashable under any circumstances. Habitual or pattern-based use of Sick Leave (for example, recurring single days adjacent to weekends) may be referred for review. 3.4 Maternity Leave (26 weeks) In accordance with the Maternity Benefit (Amendment) Act, 2017, a woman employee is entitled to 26 weeks of paid Maternity Leave for the first two surviving children, of which not more than eight weeks may be taken before the expected date of delivery. For the third child onwards, the entitlement is 12 weeks. A female employee who legally adopts a child below the age of three months, or a commissioning mother, is entitled to 12 weeks of leave from the date the child is handed over. Employees are requested to notify HR at least eight weeks in advance where the situation permits, so that maternity benefits, insurance and work-handover can be arranged. 3.5 Paternity Leave (5 days) Male employees are entitled to five working days of paid Paternity Leave, which must be availed within 30 days of the date of birth or adoption of the child. Paternity Leave may be taken as a single block or split into two instalments with prior approval. 3.6 Bereavement Leave (3 days) Three days of paid Bereavement Leave are granted on the death of an immediate family member, defined as a spouse, child, parent, sibling, or parent-in-law. Where travel to a distant location is required, the reporting manager may permit additional days to be adjusted against Earned Leave. 3.7 Optional Holidays (2 days) Employees may select two Optional Holidays per calendar year from the published restricted-holiday list, in addition to the gazetted holidays in Section 7. This allows employees to observe festivals of personal or regional significance. Optional Holidays must be applied for at least three working days in advance and cannot be carried forward. 4. Leave Application Process (Self-Service Portal) All leave must be applied for through the InfoPeople employee self-service portal (portal.infovance.com). Verbal or email-only requests will not be treated as formal applications. The standard steps are: Log in to portal.infovance.com using your single sign-on credentials. Navigate to My Workspace → Leave & Attendance → Apply for Leave. Select the leave type, start date, end date and (if part-day) the session. Enter a reason and, for Sick Leave of three or more days, attach the medical certificate. Nominate a backup colleague for work coverage where applicable. Submit. The request is routed automatically to your reporting manager and to HR. Track the status under Leave → My Requests; you will receive an email and portal notification once it is actioned. 5. Approval Workflow The standard approval flow is a two-level workflow: the reporting manager is the first approver, and Human Resources performs the final validation against leave balances and statutory entitlements. Stage Approver Action Service Level Reporting Manager Approve / reject / request modification Within 2 working days HR Operations Validate balance and statutory eligibility Within 1 working day Escalation Department Head Resolve disputes or extended leave (> 10 days) Within 3 working days If a manager does not action a request within two working days, the system sends an automated reminder; after four working days the request is auto-escalated to the Department Head. 6. Leave Without Pay (LWP) Leave Without Pay may be sanctioned only when an employee has exhausted all applicable paid-leave balances and a genuine need persists. LWP is not a matter of right and requires the written approval of both the Department Head and Human Resources. Key conditions are: LWP beyond 5 continuous days requires Department Head approval; beyond 20 days requires additional sign-off from the Head of HR. Each day of LWP results in a deduction of (Gross Salary / number of days in the month) from monthly pay. Continuous LWP exceeding 30 days may affect Provident Fund continuity, insurance cover and the annual increment eligibility for the relevant appraisal cycle. Unauthorised absence of more than 8 continuous working days without intimation may be treated as abandonment of service under the Company’s service rules. 7. Public (Gazetted) Holidays — Calendar Year 2025 The Company observes the following gazetted holidays for the Noida office. Where a holiday falls on a weekend, no compensatory off is provided unless mandated by law. # Holiday Date Day Republic Day 26 January 2025 Sunday Holi 14 March 2025 Friday Id-ul-Fitr (Ramzan Id) 31 March 2025 Monday Good Friday 18 April 2025 Friday Buddha Purnima 12 May 2025 Monday Id-ul-Zuha

Engineering Team Handbook Engineering Department - Infovance Technologies Pvt. Ltd. Document ID: ENG-HB-2025-03 Version: 6.0 Effective: 01 April 2025 Owner: Engineering Department Next review: 30 September 2025 1. About This Handbook This handbook describes how the Engineering department at Infovance Technologies builds, reviews, ships and operates software. It is the shared reference for engineers across all squads and sets the expectations for collaboration, quality and delivery. New engineers should read it in full during their first week; existing engineers should treat it as the source of truth for our ways of working. Where a client engagement mandates different practices, the client agreement takes precedence and the deviation is documented in the project’s Confluence space. 2. Team Structure The Engineering department is organised into four disciplines that collaborate within cross-functional squads aligned to products and client programmes: Frontend: Builds responsive, accessible user interfaces and design-system components. Backend: Owns APIs, business logic, data models and service integrations. DevOps / Platform: Manages CI/CD, cloud infrastructure, observability and release engineering. Quality Assurance (QA): Owns test strategy, automation, and release quality gates. Each squad is led by an Engineering Lead and supported by a Product Owner and a Scrum Master. Architectural decisions are reviewed by the Architecture Council, which maintains the technology radar and approves significant design changes. 3. Development Workflow and Git Branching We use a trunk-aware Git Flow branching model. The protected long-lived branches are main and develop; short-lived branches are created for features and fixes. Branch Rules main Production-ready code Protected; only release/hotfix merges; tagged on release develop Integration of completed work Protected; feature branches merge here via PR feature/* New features and changes Branch from develop; e.g. feature/INFV-123-login hotfix/* Urgent production fixes Branch from main; merged to main and back to develop Branch names reference the Jira ticket key (for example, feature/INFV-451-citation-api). Commits follow the Conventional Commits style (feat:, fix:, chore:, refactor:, test:, docs:) to support automated changelog generation. Force-pushing to protected branches is prohibited. 4. Code Review Process All changes reach protected branches through pull requests (PRs). A PR requires a minimum of two approvals, including at least one from a senior engineer or the discipline lead. Authors keep PRs small and focused, and provide a clear description with context, screenshots (for UI) and testing notes. Reviewers should respond within one working day. The reviewer checklist includes: Correctness: does the change do what it claims, including edge cases? Readability and maintainability; adherence to the team style guide and naming conventions. Adequate automated tests, with meaningful assertions. Security: input validation, no hard-coded secrets, safe handling of data. Performance and resource use are reasonable for the workload. No unintended scope creep; documentation and API contracts updated where relevant. 5. Sprint Ceremonies Squads operate on two-week sprints using Scrum. The standard cadence is: Ceremony When Daily Stand-up Every day, 10:00 AM (15 min) Sync on progress, plan, and blockers Sprint Planning Monday (sprint start) Commit to the sprint backlog and goals Backlog Refinement Mid-sprint Clarify and estimate upcoming work Sprint Review / Demo Last day of sprint Demo increment to stakeholders Retrospective Friday (sprint end) Reflect and agree improvement actions 6. Technology Stack Our default technology stack balances developer productivity, performance and long-term maintainability. Teams choose from the approved radar; new technologies require Architecture Council review. Frontend: React (with TypeScript), modern component libraries and a shared design system. Backend: Node.js services and Python services, exposing RESTful and event-driven APIs. Data: PostgreSQL as the primary relational store, with Redis for caching and queues. Cloud: Amazon Web Services (AWS) as the primary cloud, using managed services for compute, storage and networking. Containers: Docker images orchestrated on managed Kubernetes for production workloads. 7. Deployment Process Continuous integration and delivery are powered by Jenkins pipelines. Every merge triggers automated build, test and security scans. The promotion path is strictly staging before production: On merge to develop, artefacts are built and deployed automatically to the staging environment. QA validates the release candidate in staging against the test plan and acceptance criteria. Production deployment requires a release approval and is performed during the agreed release window. Deployments use blue-green or rolling strategies to minimise downtime. Rollback: if a production issue is detected, the release is rolled back to the last known-good version by re-pointing traffic (blue-green) or re-deploying the previous artefact; a post-incident review follows. 8. On-Call Rotation Production services are supported around the clock through an on-call rotation managed in PagerDuty. Engineers serve two-week on-call cycles, with a primary and a secondary responder. Alerts are routed by severity; the primary acknowledges within the agreed response time and engages the secondary or escalates if needed. Every page is logged, and significant incidents receive a blameless post-mortem with tracked action items. Compensatory off is provided for substantial out-of-hours engagement, in line with the Engineering on-call guidelines. 9. Documentation Standards Good documentation is part of ‘done’. Our standards are: Confluence is the home for design docs, runbooks, architecture decision records (ADRs) and onboarding material. Inline code comments explain the ‘why’ behind non-obvious logic, not the obvious ‘what’. API documentation is generated and published via Swagger / OpenAPI and kept in sync with the code. Each service maintains a README covering setup, configuration and local-run instructions. Runbooks for on-call cover common alerts, diagnostics and recovery steps. 10. Definition of Done A change is considered ‘done’ only when it meets a shared, non-negotiable bar of quality. Treating ‘done’ consistently is how we keep velocity honest and avoid hidden work piling up later. A work item is done when: Acceptance criteria in the Jira ticket are fully met and demonstrable. Code is peer-reviewed and merged through a pull request with the required approvals. Automated unit and integration tests are written and passing, and coverage gates are met. Relevant documentation, runbooks and API specs are updated. The change is deployed and verified in staging, with no open critical or high defects. Observability is in place (logs, metrics, alerts) for any new behaviour that warrants it. If any of these are incomplete, the item stays in progress rather than being marked done. Carrying partially finished work as ‘done’ distorts velocity and pushes risk into the release window. 11. Performance KPIs and Quality Gates We measure delivery health with a small set of meaningful indicators, used for improvement rather than individual Target Notes Sprint velocity (story points) Stable / predictable Tracked per squad, not per person Code coverage > 80% Enforced as a CI quality gate Escaped defect rate Low and trending down Bugs found in production per release PR cycle time Short Time from PR open to merge Change failure rate Low Percentage of deployments causing incidents Builds that fail to meet the coverage or security gates are blocked from promotion. Engineering leads review these metrics each sprint during the retrospective and agree concrete improvements. Questions about engineering practices can be raised in the #engineering channel or with your Engineering Lead.

Expense Reimbursement Policy — FY 2025-26 Finance Department - Infovance Technologies Pvt. Ltd. Document ID: FIN-POL-EXP-2025-04 Version: 5.1 Effective: 01 April 2025 Owner: Finance Department Next review: 31 March 2026 1. Purpose and Scope This Expense Reimbursement Policy defines the rules under which employees of Infovance Technologies Pvt. Ltd. may incur and reclaim legitimate business expenses for the financial year 2025-26. The objective is to ensure that employees are fairly reimbursed for costs incurred while performing their duties, while maintaining financial discipline, statutory compliance and a clear audit trail. The policy applies to all full-time employees across the Human Resources, Information Technology, Finance and Engineering departments. Contractors and vendors are reimbursed strictly as per their contractual terms. All expenses must be reasonable, directly related to Company business, supported by valid documentation, and within the limits set out below. Employees are expected to exercise the same prudence with Company money as they would with their own. 2. Eligible Expense Categories The following categories of expense are reimbursable when incurred wholly for business purposes: Travel: Local commuting for official work, and outstation/inter-city travel by approved modes (rail AC chair car / 3AC, economy-class air for journeys over six hours or where time-critical, and metered/app-based cabs). Accommodation: Hotel stay during outstation business travel. Meals: Reasonable meal expenses during business travel or while working at a client site away from base location. Client Entertainment: Pre-approved meals or hospitality with clients in furtherance of business, with the client name and purpose recorded. 3. Expense Limits The following standard limits apply. Amounts above these limits require explicit prior approval from the Department Head and will be assessed case by case. Category Limit Notes Local travel (auto-approved) INR 500 / day App-based cab or auto receipts; no separate sign-off up to limit Outstation accommodation INR 3,000 / day Metro cities; tariff inclusive of taxes Meals INR 800 / day During business travel or client-site deputation Client entertainment INR 2,500 / event Prior manager approval; record client & purpose International travel Separate approval Requires CFO sign-off and a pre-trip budget Limits are caps, not entitlements. Where the actual reasonable cost is lower, only the actual cost is reimbursed. Accommodation in non-metro locations is generally expected to be lower than the metro cap. 4. International Travel International business travel requires a separate, pre-approved travel requisition and budget, signed off by the Department Head and the Chief Financial Officer. Per-diem rates, visa costs, foreign-exchange arrangements and travel insurance are handled through the dedicated international-travel process administered by the Finance team. Personal travel combined with a business trip must be clearly demarcated, with personal costs borne by the employee. 5. Submission Process All claims are submitted through the InfoPeople portal under Finance → Reimbursements. The process is: Submit each claim within 30 days of the date the expense was incurred. Claims older than 30 days require a written justification and Department Head approval, and may be rejected. Create a claim, select the category, enter the amount and date, and attach legible supporting documents. Group related expenses from a single trip under one claim reference for easier review. Submit for approval; the claim is routed automatically through the approval workflow in Section 7. Track status under My Reimbursements; you will be notified of approval, query or rejection. 6. Required Documentation Valid documentation is mandatory for every claim. Reimbursement cannot be processed without it. Acceptable Original tax invoices or bills showing the vendor name, GSTIN (where applicable), date and amount. For air travel: the e-ticket, boarding passes and the airline tax invoice. For rail travel: the ticket and reservation confirmation. For cabs/autos: the trip receipt or app invoice. For hotels: the itemised GST tax invoice (not merely the booking confirmation). For client entertainment: the bill plus a note of attendees, client name and business purpose. Hand-written chits, screenshots without invoice details, or estimated amounts are not acceptable. Where a GST invoice is available, it must be obtained to enable input tax credit (see Section 9). 7. Approval Workflow and Authority Limits Claim Value (per claim) Approving Authority Up to INR 10,000 Reporting Manager INR 10,001 to INR 50,000 Department Head Above INR 50,000 Chief Financial Officer (CFO) Each approver validates the business justification, policy compliance and supporting documents. Claims may be returned with queries; employees should respond promptly to avoid delays. 8. Payment Timeline Approved reimbursements are paid directly to the employee’s registered bank account within seven working days of final approval, through the next available payment run. Reimbursements are paid separately from monthly salary and are reflected in the employee’s reimbursement statement on the portal. 9. GST Input Tax Credit eligible input tax credit, employees must obtain proper tax invoices bearing the Company’s GSTIN and registered name wherever the expense is incurred for business and the vendor is GST-registered. This is particularly relevant for hotels, air travel and client meals. Claims accompanied by a compliant GST invoice are processed faster; the Finance team reconciles eligible credit against the vendor’s GST filings. Input credit is generally not available on certain blocked categories under the GST law, and Finance will advise where this applies. 10. Travel Advance For significant outstation or international travel, an employee may request a travel advance to cover anticipated costs. Raise a travel-advance request on the portal with the trip dates, purpose and an estimated budget, at least five working days before travel. On approval, the advance is credited to the registered bank account before the trip. Within 15 days of returning, submit the actual expense claim with bills to settle the advance. Any unspent balance must be refunded to the Company; any approved excess is reimbursed. Failure to settle an advance within 30 days may result in recovery from salary. 11. Non-Reimbursable Expenses The following are not reimbursable under any circumstances: Personal expenses (personal shopping, personal phone bills, personal entertainment). Traffic fines, penalties, late-payment charges and parking fines. Alcohol, except where pre-approved as part of a sanctioned client-entertainment event. In-room mini-bar, laundry beyond reasonable limits, spa and recreation charges. Upgrades to business/first class without prior CFO approval. Loss of personal belongings during travel. Expenses without valid supporting documentation. Expenses incurred on behalf of family members or non-employees. 12. Compliance and Audit Expense claims are subject to random and periodic audit by the Finance team and internal auditors. Submission of false, inflated or duplicate claims is a serious breach of the Code of Conduct and may lead to disciplinary action, recovery of amounts and, in serious cases, termination. For clarifications, contact finance@infovance.com. This policy is reviewed annually and supersedes all prior expense guidelines.

Post-Incident Report — VPN Outage IT Team (Infrastructure & Support) - Infovance Technologies Pvt. Ltd. Document ID: IR-2026-0106-VPN Version: 1.0 Effective: 06 January 2026 Owner: IT Team (Infra & Support) Next review: Monthly IT Ops Review Incident: Corporate VPN Outage — Gateway Certificate Expiry • Severity: P1 (Critical) • Status: Resolved / Closed Date of incident: 06 January 2026 • Prepared by: Vikram Reddy (IT Manager) • Reviewed by: Information Security & Risk 1. Summary On the morning of 06 January 2026, the corporate VPN gateway (vpn.infovance.com) became unavailable, preventing remote employees from establishing Cisco AnyConnect sessions. The outage lasted approximately 3 hours and affected an estimated 200 remote-working employees across all four departments. The root cause was an expired TLS server certificate on the VPN gateway. Service was restored after the certificate was renewed and the gateway services were reloaded. There was no evidence of any security breach or data loss. 2. Incident Timeline (IST) Time Event 08:05 First user reports of ‘connection failed’ on AnyConnect arrive via helpdesk@infovance.com and Jira Service Desk. 08:15 Helpdesk observes a spike in identical VPN tickets; issue flagged to Infrastructure on-call. 08:25 Incident declared P1 (Critical) — many users, no workaround, business-wide remote access impacted. 08:30 IT confirms the gateway is reachable but rejecting TLS handshakes; error points to an invalid/expired certificate. 08:45 Root cause confirmed: VPN gateway certificate expired at 00:00 on 06 Jan; auto-renewal had not fired. 09:10 Replacement certificate issued and validated. 09:40 Certificate installed on the gateway; staged config check. 10:05 VPN services reloaded; test connections from IT succeed. 10:25 Phased confirmation — users asked to reconnect. 11:05 Ticket volume back to baseline; incident marked resolved. 11:30 Comms sent confirming restoration; PIR scheduled. 3. Root Cause The TLS server certificate on the primary VPN gateway expired. The certificate was on a manual/auto-renew arrangement that failed silently — the scheduled renewal job did not execute and no expiry alert was configured, so the certificate lapsed unnoticed. When clients attempted the TLS handshake, the gateway presented an expired certificate, which AnyConnect rejected, blocking all new VPN sessions. Existing long-lived sessions were unaffected until they renegotiated, which is why impact appeared gradually rather than all at once. 4. Impact Assessment Affected users: ~200 remote employees unable to connect to internal systems for the duration of the outage. Duration: ~3 hours (approx. 08:05 – 11:05 IST). Departments affected: HR, IT, Finance, Engineering (remote staff). Business impact: Delayed access to internal applications, timesheets and code repositories for remote workers. On-site staff unaffected. No client-facing production systems went down; no SLA breaches reported. Data/security impact: None. No unauthorized access, data loss or breach — an availability-only failure. 5. Resolution Steps Identified the expired gateway certificate as the cause. Issued and validated a replacement TLS certificate. Installed the new certificate on the VPN gateway. Reloaded gateway services and verified the TLS handshake. Confirmed successful test connections from the IT team. Asked employees to reconnect; monitored ticket volume to baseline before declaring resolution. Sent an all-clear communication to staff. 6. Preventive Measures / Action Items Action Owner Due Automated certificate expiry monitoring + alerting for all gateways and critical services (alerts at 30/14/7 days) IT Infrastructure 23 Jan 2026 Maintain a central certificate inventory with owners and expiry dates; review monthly IT Infrastructure 31 Jan 2026 Verify and harden the auto-renewal job; add a failure alert that pages on-call IT Infrastructure 23 Jan 2026 Add a VPN-down runbook to Confluence and link it in the on-call rotation IT Support 30 Jan 2026 Staff reminder: use helpdesk/Service Desk (not DMs); report security incidents to security@infovance.com within 2 hours IT Manager Comms at all-hands 7. Sign-off Prepared by Vikram Reddy (IT Manager); approved by Information Security & Risk. Lessons learned to be reviewed at the next monthly IT operations meeting.

Holiday and Festival Working Engineering and IT personnel rostered for production support or on-call duty during a gazetted holiday will be granted compensatory off, to be availed within 30 days, subject to manager approval and recorded through the self-service portal. 9. Advance Leave In exceptional circumstances, an employee who has been confirmed in service may be granted Earned Leave in advance of accrual, up to a maximum of five days, against future accrual. Advance Leave requires Department Head approval and will be adjusted automatically against subsequent monthly accruals. 10. Queries and Contact For clarifications regarding leave balances, encashment calculations, statutory entitlements or portal issues, employees may contact the Human Resources team at hr@infovance.com or visit the HR Operations desk on the 4th floor during working hours. This policy supersedes all previous leave policies and is subject to annual review.
